title: The page was never bare
checkLabels.t4: The margin around the body is gone
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the file instead of adding rules. Hit the bin and start again.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. You haven't taken anything off yet.
hint2.untouched: You write in the styles.css tab. index.html isn't touched in this lesson.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed: If you've edited index.html, hit the bin. The work is only in styles.css.
why.unlinked: The page can't find styles.css any more. You've edited index.html.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin to restore both files, and work only in styles.css.
why.no-body: There is no body. You've edited index.html.
why.no-h1: There is no heading. You've edited index.html — the work is in the other file.
why.no-ul: There is no list. You've edited index.html.
why.no-a: There is no link. You've edited index.html.
why.margin-left: The text still doesn't reach the edge. There's a margin you never wrote — it comes from the browser.
hint2.margin-left: The margin is around the whole body of the page. So the rule is for body.
hint3.margin-left: Every browser puts about 8 pixels around the content so it doesn't stick to the edge. That's why the first rule in almost every real site removes it.
why.h1-still-big: The heading is still larger than the text. Nobody wrote that it should be — the browser decided.
hint2.h1-still-big: The paragraph is 16 pixels. Make the heading the same and see what's left of it.
hint3.h1-still-big: A heading defaults to twice the text size. Not because h1 means large, but because someone thirty years ago picked 2em and everyone copied it.
hint4.h1-still-big: In a rule for h1 set font-size: 16px.
why.bullets-left: The list items still have discs in front of them. You didn't put those there either.
hint2.bullets-left: The property is called list-style-type. Look for a value that means no marker.
hint3.bullets-left: Every navigation menu on the web is a ul. Not one of them has discs — because every one removed them exactly like this.
hint4.bullets-left: In a rule for ul set list-style-type: none.
why.underline-left: The link is still underlined. That's the browser's decision too, not yours.
hint3.underline-left: Careful here. An underline on a link isn't decoration — it's the only thing that tells a link apart from text for someone who can't tell the colours apart. Take it off and you owe them another signal.
hint4.underline-left: In a rule for a set text-decoration: none. And remember what you lost.
why.lost-li: A list item is gone. The work is in styles.css.
blocks.0.text: In lesson 01 I wrote that this is what a page without CSS looks like. That wasn't true, and now you'll prove it yourself.
blocks.1.text: The page on the right has exactly one rule — the font. And still: the heading is large, the list has discs, the link is blue and underlined, everything sits away from the edge. Who wrote that?
blocks.3.text: Every browser carries its own CSS file and applies it before yours ever arrives. It decides that:
blocks.4.items.0: h1 is twice the size of the text
blocks.4.items.3: the body sits away from the edge
blocks.6.text: So that a page whose author wrote no styles is still readable. Open a scientific document from 1998 — it has headings, paragraphs, lists. It works. That's insurance, not aesthetics.
blocks.7.text: And something important follows: you don't start from nothing. You start from someone else's decision that you've never seen, and sometimes your first job is to take it off.
blocks.8.text: That's why almost every real site opens with a few lines that remove the defaults. They're called a reset or a normalize. Now you'll write your own, a small one.
blocks.10.text: In styles.css take off four things: the margin around the body, the large heading, the discs on the list, and the underline on the link. Don't touch index.html.
blocks.11.text: When it passes — look at the preview. That's a page truly without styles. It's ugly in a different way, and now you know exactly why.
